candidates.why: Primary top-line revenue line; preferred over duplicate total.
Duplicate total revenue line with identical values; useful as fallback across years.

candidates.row_label: Total comprehensive income attributable to the owners of the Group
Total comprehensive income for the year

candidates.why: Balance sheet liability, not top-line revenue.

variables.notes: Using revenue lines as a priority list; later code should take the first non-missing value.

R:c. No candidate list provided for COGS. The statements do not present an explicit 'Cost of sales' line; for a shipping company, 'Voyage Expenses' appears to be the closest direct service cost line. 'Vessel operating expenses' may also be direct operating cost, but to keep the outside-candidate choice minimal, selected only the strongest single proxy.

candidates.why: Looks like direct operating/service cost for vessels rather than SG&A overhead.
Explicit SG&A-style overhead row; best match under selection hierarchy.
Could be a component of G&A in some years, but explicit G&A row exists so subtotal/header row should be preferred alone.

variables.notes: Selected the explicit SG&A row only, per hierarchy rule. Excluded 'Employee Costs' to avoid double counting because it appears to be a component within G&A. Excluded 'Vessel operating expenses' as direct operating cost, not overhead.
No dedicated research and development expense line appears on the income statement.

candidates.why: Best available recurring finance-cost line; preferable to balance-sheet payable and to one-off finance cost items.

candidates.row_label: Finance costs related to covenants of early termination
candidates.why: Clearly non-recurring/specific item; should be excluded.
Balance-sheet accrual, not period interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest and other finance costs - Balancing value
candidates.why: Balancing row should not be preferred over the reported finance cost line.

variables.notes: Used the reported finance cost total because granular recurring interest components are not separately available in a clean way. Excluded one-off and balancing rows.

candidates.why: Best available equity line; no separate non-controlling interest line is shown.
Equivalent total equity line; useful fallback if label changes across years.
Total balance sheet, not book equity.

variables.notes: No owner-vs-NCI split is presented; using equity lines as priority list. Since no minority interest line exists, MIB is left empty to avoid double counting.
No minority interest/non-controlling interest line appears on the balance sheet.
